title: Jacobaea acutipinna
taxonomy: Jacobaea acutipinna is classified as a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Africa. It belongs to the genus Jacobaea, which is a group of plants that are often known as ragworts or Jacob's ladders. The genus Jacobaea is sometimes included in the genus Senecio, but it is generally considered to be a separate group. The classification of Jacobaea acutipinna reflects its distinct characteristics and evolutionary history within the sunflower family.

intro: Jacobaea acutipinna, also known as Green Hesperis, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiproliferative effects. The bioactive constituents of Jacobaea acutipinna include sesquiterpene lactones, such as heliangolide and 6β-Hydroxy Jacobin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Jacobaea acutipinna include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of infections and inflammatory conditions.
